mentions.note: Likes company very much; down 30% from highs; but big buyer of Micron/storage at inflated prices; margins being hurt; wait for peak in storage costs or earnings reports.
Down 30% from 3-month high; low tech PE ratio; BUT big buyer of Micron/storage with prices rising—margins getting hit; wait for earnings or storage peak before buying.
Memory cost inflation eroding customer margins; rising memory prices directly hurt Dell's profitability and pricing power.
Memory chip cost spikes eating into margins; PC/server customer suffering as suppliers raise prices.
Data center play up 29.4% in February; huge Q4 beat on AI product sales and margins; strong backlog and bullish full-year outlook; rallied 22% Friday alone.

45% EPS growth, $64B AI orders, $43B backlog; Dell AI factory serving 4,000+ customers; financing moat (Dell Financial Services) unique advantage; up 24% YTD, 64% past 12 months; still outperforming Mag 7 (ex-Nvidia).
Growing incredibly with huge pipeline in on-prem AI compute; better value than Super Micro in ecosystem buildout.
Key customer connector for enterprises adopting Nvidia accelerated computing; most undervalued stock in market; only if it retreats from current levels.

High-quality company wrongly hated during free fall from $168 to $110; strong fundamentals (vendor financing moat, best book of business, dividend raise, $10B buyback hike) justified pyramid buying; missed by charitable trust but accessible to individual investors.
Better choice than Super Micro; Michael Dell excellent management; data center AI demand strong.
Data center hardware provider; Cramer asks 'How did I miss Dell?'; selling servers and infrastructure to hyperscalers at scale.
Parabolic move; Cramer wishes trust owned more but stock has run too far and needs correction for healthy market.
Server manufacturer; builds AI factories; essential infrastructure for data center construction.
Wish bought +100 pts ago for travel trust; infrastructure beneficiary of AI boom; quality comppounder with temporary headwinds.
Data center infrastructure supplier; major player in AI buildout ecosystem.
CEO Michael Dell's compelling AI and accelerated computing growth story was too good to pass up; missed opportunity from $156 to $305; hardware winner in new era.
Massive 32% single-session gain on blowout quarter powered by Nvidia AI server demand; 'defined today' and created tech strength catalyst
Data center compute supplier benefiting from AI infrastructure buildout; up 233% YTD; analyst price target leapfrog continuing.
Taking business from IBM at ~150 to now 457; trades 25x earnings but estimates likely too low; benefiting from supply constraints, corporate hardware spending surge.
Down nearly 10% despite best numbers and potential market share gains from IBM; component stock collapse suggests market stress despite fundamentals.
